commit.message:
fix(rustc-codegen): fix sret slot reuse, large struct copy, fat pointer refs, and float intrinsics

call.rs - sret slot reuse: when the call destination is a pre-allocated stack
local, reuse the existing slot. MIR blocks are translated in numeric order so
use-sites may be lowered before the call site; all must share the same slot.

call.rs - large struct copy: when passing a struct >16 bytes (held in a
stack slot pointer) as a by-value argument, copy it to a fresh temporary.
Passing the original slot would allow the callee to mutate the caller's local,
violating Rust pass-by-value semantics.

call.rs - sret projection: when a sret function writes its result to a stack
slot and the MIR destination is a field projection, memcpy from the sret slot
to the projected address instead of incorrectly storing the return VReg.

statement.rs - fat pointer ref with deep projection: when taking a reference
to (*_1).field (projection length > 1), the pre-computed  already holds
the correctly offset pointer. Use the base local's fat metadata with
rather than blindly copying the whole stack slot, which stored the unoffset
base pointer.

intrinsic.rs - minnumf32/minnumf64/maxnumf32/maxnumf64: implement using
FCmp + Select so that NaN-propagation matches the intrinsic contract.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(legalize): fix wide_pair sign/zero extension and add 128-bit to float conversion

wide_pair(): non-wide 128-bit operands (constants that fit in 64 bits) now
correctly derive the hi word based on annotation:
- Unsigned annotation: hi = 0 (zero-extend), since a u64 value's 128-bit
  form always has zero upper bits even when bit 63 is set.
- Signed or unannotated: hi = sar(lo, 63) (sign-extend), handling negative
  constants like iconst(-1) used as all-ones masks in bitwise operations.

leg_int128_to_fp(): legalize i128/u128 to f32/f64 conversions that LLVM lowers
via runtime helper calls. Split the wide value into (lo, hi) parts and
reconstruct the float via ldexpf/ldexp arithmetic on the parts.

9–15 byte loads: split into an 8-byte lo load and a (bytes-8)-byte hi load
to produce a correct (lo, hi) Pair for downstream wide stores.

Wide store passthrough: for store.N (N >= 16) where the value comes directly
from a load of the same width, pass the full value through without truncation.

commit.message:
feat(x86): add AdcRR/SbbRR, size-aware count ops, float calling convention, icmp/min/max size fixes, partial load/store

AdcRR/SbbRR: add-with-carry and subtract-with-borrow for 128-bit arithmetic.

Popcnt/Lzcnt/Tzcnt size field: previously hardcoded 64-bit, producing wrong
counts on narrower values. Now emit the correct operand-size prefix so that
e.g. ctlz(u8) counts zeros in an 8-bit value (via 16-bit promotion).

gen_icmp: use correct comparison width derived from operand annotation instead
of always comparing 64-bit. Emit SetCC+MovzxB immediately using separate VRegs
(tmp_cc for SetCC, dst for MovzxB) to prevent spill-slot corruption when the
allocator spills between the two instructions.

gen_min/gen_max: pass the correct operand size instead of always using 64-bit,
fixing wrong min/max results on i8/i16/i32 types.

Float calling convention (SysV x86-64): classify parameters into GP vs XMM
classes independently. Float args go to xmm0–xmm7 regardless of how many
GP args precede them; float return values come back in xmm0.

emit_partial_load/emit_partial_store: load/store non-power-of-2 byte counts
(3, 5, 6, 7) by splitting into standard-size accesses to avoid reads past the
intended range.

commit.message:
fix(target): add secondary_map to VRegMap to prevent aliasing of primary and secondary results

VRegMap used a single flat array indexed by val.index(), which strips the
SECONDARY_BIT tag. This caused secondary instruction results to overwrite
their corresponding primary result's VReg assignment, corrupting any
subsequent use of the primary value.

Add a dedicated secondary_map array that stores VReg assignments for
secondary results separately from primary ones. Dispatch assign() and get()
to the correct array based on val.is_secondary_result().

commit.message:
Consolidate .NET SDK versions to 10.0.x across all GitHub workflows (#5847)

## Description

Eight workflows install 2+ .NET SDK versions (combinations of 6.0.x,
8.0.x, 9.0.x alongside 10.0.x). All secondary versions are unnecessary —
.NET 10 SDK resolves older targeting packs via NuGet, self-contained
publishes bundle the runtime, and tools like CLOG/docfx support
roll-forward.

**Workflow changes** — remove secondary `dotnet-version` entries,
keeping only `10.0.x`:

- `check-clog.yml`: drop 8.0.x (CLOG already sets
`DOTNET_ROLL_FORWARD=Major`)
- `publish-docfx.yml`: drop 8.x (global tools roll forward natively)
- `check-dotnet.yml`, `dotnet-test.yml`, `plugins.yml`: drop 6.0.x (SDK
builds net6.0/netcoreapp3.1/netstandard targets via NuGet targeting
packs)
- `package-linux.yml`, `package-alpine-linux.yml`,
`validate-linux-packages-reuse.yml`: drop 9.0.x (self-contained publish
includes runtime in output)
- `plugins.yml`: add `DOTNET_ROLL_FORWARD: LatestMajor` to the Package
step so the `plugintool` global tool (which targets net6.0) runs on the
.NET 10 runtime instead of requiring .NET 6 to be installed.

**Project change:**

- `src/cs/tool/MsQuicTool.csproj`: `net6.0` → `net10.0`. This is an
internal test tool (not published), and `dotnet run` requires a matching
runtime. The library `msquic.csproj` multi-targeting
(net6.0/netstandard2.0/netstandard2.1) is unchanged.

## Testing

All existing CI workflows cover these changes — they exercise the exact
build/run paths affected. No new tests needed.

## Documentation

No documentation impact.

commit.message:
Add relocation offset bounds checking

Implements critical security fix to prevent out-of-bounds memory writes
during relocation processing.

Changes:
- Added upper bound check in applyRelaTable() for relocation offsets
- Validates that r.Offset < BaseVAddr + MemSize before processing
- Created bounds_test.go with build tag '!race' for offset validation tests
- Test excluded from race builds due to checkptr limitations with test data

The bounds check ensures relocation offsets are within the mapped memory
range, preventing write-out-of-bounds vulnerabilities from malicious ELF
files specifying offsets beyond allocated segments.

Test coverage:
- TestRelocationOffsetBoundsChecking verifies offsets are rejected:
  * Offset before BaseVAddr
  * Offset at exact upper boundary
  * Offset beyond upper boundary

Fix GoServer and GoClient Docker builds by running go mod tidy (#2160)

PR #2107 (Aug 2025) updated golang.org/x/net from v0.33.0 to v0.38.0 in
go.mod but did not update go.sum, causing `go build` inside Docker to
fail with "missing go.sum entry for module providing package
golang.org/x/net/...".

This is the same issue that was fixed for BasicGoServer in PR #2149.

Running `go mod tidy` also:
- Updates go directive from 1.21 to 1.23.0 with toolchain go1.23.7
- Removes unused github.com/golang/glog dependency
- Updates indirect dependencies (x/sys 0.28.0->0.31.0, x/text 0.21.0->0.23.0)

feat: add 5 automated guard checks to prevent recurring documentation drift

Root cause analysis identified 4 failure modes from this session's 120+ fixes:
1. Rename propagation failure (convention changes miss downstream refs)
2. Relative path fragility (broken markdown links across directory levels)
3. Hardcoded values that drift (commit hashes, line numbers)
4. Information duplication without sync enforcement

New validation checks (7-11):
- Check 7: Convention linter — scans for stale 'command:' format and CLAUDE.md refs
- Check 8: Markdown link validator — resolves relative paths, flags broken targets
- Check 9: Trigger keyword sync — verifies helpers registered in AGENT.md
- Check 10: AGENT.md deployment freshness — compares source vs deployed copy
- Check 11: Anti-hardcoding guard — detects commit hashes in mechanism files

Also fixes last broken link: adding_new_crop.md ../core_docs/ → ../../core_docs/

Validation now runs 26 checks (up from 20), covering every bug class found this session.

commit.message:
Party mode  (#2911)

* feat: add generic short code authentication system

Add a reusable short code authentication system that any provider can use
for QR code login, device pairing, or similar flows.

Changes:
- Add join_codes database table (schema v6)
- Add generate_join_code(user_id, provider_name, ...) method
- Add exchange_join_code() to convert codes to JWT tokens
- Add auth/code public API endpoint
- Add revoke_join_codes() for cleanup
- Update login.html to handle ?join= parameter
- Add provider_name parameter to JWT token encoding

Providers can implement short code auth flows like:

    code, expires = await auth.generate_join_code(
        user_id=my_user.user_id,
        provider_name="my_provider",
        expires_in_hours=24,
    )

The provider_name is stored in the join code and passed to the JWT token,
allowing providers to identify their authenticated sessions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add queue modifier hook system for provider-driven queue behavior

Introduces a generic QueueModifier protocol that allows providers to
customize queue behavior without hardcoding provider-specific logic
in the core controller.

Providers can register queue modifiers to:
- Modify enqueue options (e.g., convert ADD to PLAY)
- Calculate custom insert indexes for items
- Control whether items should be shuffled
- Protect certain items from being shuffled

This enables features like party mode's guest priority queue to be
implemented entirely in the provider rather than core.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add user tracking to queue items

Track which user added each item to the queue via extra_attributes:
- added_by_user_id: The user who added the item
- added_by_user_role: The role of that user (for priority logic)
- queue_option: How the item was added ("play", "next", "add")

This enables provider hooks (like party mode's guest priority queue)
to make decisions based on who added which items.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(party_mode): implement QueueModifier for guest priority queue

Adds the Party Mode plugin provider that uses the generic queue modifier
system to implement guest priority queue behavior:

- Registers as a QueueModifier on load
- Implements modify_enqueue_option to auto-start playback for guests
- Implements calculate_insert_index for guest priority insertion
- Implements should_shuffle_items to prevent guest item shuffling
- Implements get_protected_item_ids to protect guest items during shuffle

Also includes:
- Guest user management (party_guest account)
- Join code generation using generic short code auth
- Rate limiting configuration for queue actions
- Configurable UI options (album art background, player controls)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update GENERIC_PROVIDER_FEATURES.md with queue modifier hooks

- Updated section 3 to document the QueueModifier protocol
- Added registration and implementation examples
- Updated branch structure diagram
- Updated PR strategy with current status

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Revise db migration

* fix: walrus operator falsy-zero bug in queue modifier insert index

calculate_insert_index returning 0 (insert at beginning) was silently
ignored because the walrus operator treats 0 as falsy. Use explicit
`is not None` check instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: make calculate_insert_index use first-modifier-wins like modify_enqueue_option

The modify_enqueue_option loop breaks after the first modifier returns
a value, but calculate_insert_index let every modifier overwrite the
result so the last one silently won. Guard the call with
`if custom_insert_index is None` so the first modifier to return
a value is authoritative, matching the enqueue option loop semantics.
A break is not used because the same loop also checks
should_shuffle_items for all modifiers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: atomic use-count check in exchange_join_code to prevent TOCTOU race

Replace separate read/check/update of join code use_count with a single
atomic SQL UPDATE that combines the max_uses guard and increment, preventing
concurrent requests from exceeding the use limit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: pass existing queue items through QueueModifier protocol

_find_guest_section_end was reaching into the private
player_queues._queue_items dict. Add existing_items parameter to the
calculate_insert_index protocol method so the controller passes the
data through instead of the provider accessing private internals.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove dead _is_jwt_token() static method from auth

The method was never called anywhere in the codebase.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: scope revoke_join_codes to provider instead of deleting all codes

revoke_join_codes() previously deleted ALL join codes system-wide when
called without a user_id. Add provider_name filter parameter so
providers can revoke only their own codes. Party mode now passes
provider_name="party_mode" to avoid nuking codes from other providers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: add explicit multi_instance and builtin fields to party mode manifest

Aligns with other providers that explicitly declare these fields
rather than relying on defaults.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add comprehensive tests for join code authentication

Add tests covering the short code authentication system:
- generate_join_code: basic functionality, invalid user handling
- exchange_join_code: success, case-insensitivity, expired codes,
  max_uses limits, unlimited uses, provider_name in JWT claims
- revoke_join_codes: per-user revocation, revoke all codes
- authenticate_with_join_code API: success and error cases

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: use provider API commands for party mode queue operations

Instead of using QueueModifier hooks in the core controller, the party
mode provider now registers custom API commands (party_mode/add_to_queue,
party_mode/skip) that handle all guest queue behavior internally.

This keeps party-mode-specific logic in the provider and avoids changes
to core controllers. The provider marks queue items with extra_attributes
(party_mode_guest, party_mode_boosted) for frontend display.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use queue item ID comparison for reliable extra_attributes marking

The previous URI matching approach was unreliable because play_media()
resolves the URI to a media item, which may have a different URI format.
Now we compare queue item IDs before/after adding to reliably find and
mark the new item(s) with party_mode_guest and party_mode_boosted.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: implement guest priority queue and boost section for party mode

Guest items now have priority over regular queue items:
- Regular guest adds go after other guest items but before playlist items
- Boosted items go after other boosted items but before regular guest items

Queue order: currently playing → boosted → regular guest → playlist

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: disambiguation no longer re-triggers for existing profiles

_resolve_from_disambiguation always returned already_exists=False and
needs_research=[canonical_title], forcing every post-disambiguation turn
through the async research path. If the user sent another message before
the background task finished, profile_resolved was still False and the
whole intent resolution re-ran — triggering a fresh disambiguation.

Fix: before building the synthetic IntentResolution, check whether a
profile already exists on disk (al_{anilist_id}.yaml first, then
find_profile_by_title fallback). If found, set already_exists=True and
needs_research=[] with the real on-disk profile_id.

This lets the caller's 'if not needs_research and existing_profiles' branch
fire synchronously, setting profile_resolved=True before the session is
saved — eliminating the race condition entirely.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
